channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Iran Gained Leverage in the War
item.description: Outmatched militarily, Iran used “triangular coercion” by attacking Gulf states and closing the Strait of Hormuz. It points to a long-term U.S. vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Enghelab Square in Tehran in April showing American military hardware trapped in a net.

item.title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
item.description: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran registering as a candidate in the presidential election in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
item.description: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The sliver of G.O.P. skepticism to the president’s handling of the Iran conflict widened last week.

item.title: Israeli Minister Threatens to Evict Palestinians From West Bank Hamlet
item.description: Bezalel Smotrich, the hard-line minister, said he was retaliating for the International Criminal Court prosecutor seeking his arrest, something the court did not confirm nor deny.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: The Israeli finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich, in Jerusalem earlier this month.

item.title: Inflation Fears Cloud G7 Economic Agenda as Iran War Persists
item.description: The United States and Europe were at odds over the Trump administration’s decision to ease oil sanctions on Russia.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Says He Postponed a ‘Very Major Attack’ on Iran, Leaving Mideast on Edge
item.description: President Trump threatened “another big hit” on Iran, saying the country had days to return to the negotiating table, as Pakistan continued its mediation efforts to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump said the U.S. military was prepared for a “full, large scale assault” against Iran.

item.title: Here’s How Iran Could Respond to Renewed U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: In any new round of fighting, Iranian officials could adopt new tactics, including intensified strikes on neighbors and trying to close off a second strait.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Iranians have used their proximity to the Strait of Hormuz, a critical trade waterway, to exert immense pressure on the global economy.

item.title: Trump Threatens Iran and Then Pulls Back, All in the Same Day
item.description: President Trump has repeatedly said he’ll restart military action against Iran, only to stop short of plunging the United States directly back into an unpopular war.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A U.S. military official said that Iran had been able to study the flight patterns of American fighter jets and bombers.

item.title: Trump Warns Iran the ‘Clock Is Ticking’ as Tensions Surge Again in Middle East
item.description: The president has sought to force Iran to accept his terms on its nuclear program or else face renewed war. An emboldened Iran has rebuffed Trump’s demands.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump on Friday. He said in a social media post on Sunday that Iran had to move fast “or there won’t be anything left,” adding “the Clock is Ticking.”

item.title: Blocked Strait of Hormuz Leads to New Opportunities for Syria
item.description: The latest war in the Middle East has created new economic opportunities for Syria thanks to its geography.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iraqi trucks carrying oil along the highway near the Baniyas port and refinery in Syria in April.

item.title: G7 Finance Ministers Look to Contain Iran Economic Fallout
item.description: Top policymakers were expected to discuss rising energy prices and sanctions policy at a critical summit in Paris this week.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, greeting Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent last week in Beijing. Mr. Bessent is leading the U.S. delegation at the G7 finance ministers’ meeting in Paris this week.

item.title: The Iran War Is Crippling One of the World’s Wealthiest Nations
item.description: Iranian attacks and the stoppage of seaborne transit have paralyzed Qatar’s vital gas exports, stalling the economic pivots intended to anchor the country’s growth.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Qatar has tried to transform itself into a tourist destination and a hub for international business and finance.

item.title: Fears Grow That Iran May Be Using Proxy Groups Beyond Mideast
item.description: The charges against Mohammad al-Saadi in the United States have raised concerns that Iran could be working with its proxies to stage attacks outside the region.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Neil MacFarquhar and Falih Hassan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The funeral of a commander from the Kataib Hezbollah paramilitary group in Baghdad in 2024. The group is backed by Iran, and its fighters have killed American soldiers and kidnapped people in the past.

item.title: They Fled to Safety in Palestinian Territory, Then Settlers Attacked Again
item.description: Violent settlers are not merely clearing Palestinians from land under Israel’s control. They are attacking areas where Israel agreed to Palestinian self-governance.
item.dc:creator: Azam Ahmed and Ivor Prickett
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ivor Prickett for The New York Times

item.title: Which Countries Are Profiting From the Iran War Oil Shock
item.description: An analysis of oil export data offers clues about which nations have benefited from higher prices, and which have lost a lot of revenue.
item.dc:creator: Amy Fan and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Kataib Hezbollah: What to Know
item.description: A commander of one of the most-hard line and powerful Iranian proxies in Iraq has been charged with plotting to attack Jewish sites in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A protester holding a Kataib Hezbollah flag in Baghdad, Iraq, in March. The militia was founded after the 2003 U.S. invasion of Iraq.

item.title: Hamas’s Top Leader in Gaza Is Killed in Israeli Strike
item.description: Izz al-Din al-Haddad took over the group’s military wing in Gaza last year. Hamas officials confirmed Mr. al-Haddad’s death in an Israeli attack.
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas, Jonathan Rosen and Iyad Abuheweila

item.title: Back From China, Trump Faces Decision on Whether to Resume Strikes on Iran
item.description: Top aides have drafted battle plans as peace negotiations have stalled.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Eric Schmitt and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump has not yet decided whether to resume military strikes on Iran, but Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told lawmakers that the Pentagon had “a plan to escalate if necessary.”

item.title: Executions Surge in Iran Since Cease-Fire, Rights Groups Say
item.description: Many were detained during January’s mass protests, according to a rights group, amid concerns the authorities are trying to intimidate Iranians from returning to the streets.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader who was killed in an airstrike in February, pictured on a billboard in Tehran this month.

item.title: Republicans Waited to Challenge Trump on the Iran War. Now It May Be Too Late.
item.description: Having deferred to the president for months, G.O.P. lawmakers missed crucial milestones to try to limit his war powers. That has tied their hands in seeking parameters and exit criteria.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Senator Lisa Murkowski, Republican of Alaska, during a Senate appropriation hearing last month.

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as Trump-Xi Summit Yields No Clear Breakthroughs on Iran War
item.description: Hopes for an end to the war in Iran faded after President Trump failed to secure a commitment from China to help persuade Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Aging Palestinian Leader Boosting His Son’s Political Rise, Officials Say
item.description: Mahmoud Abbas’s years in power have been dogged by accusations of corruption. Many Palestinians yearn for fresh leadership.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:description: Mahmoud Abbas, president of the Palestinian Authority, center, appearing at a Fatah conference in the Israeli-occupied West Bank city of Ramallah on Thursday.

item.title: A Top U.S. Commander Dismisses Reports of Civilian Deaths in Iran
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said a strike on an Iranian school may have been the only civilian casualty incident of the war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Admiral Cooper told the lawmakers that his staff had been proactive in trying to prevent civilian deaths. “Our staff specifically warned the Iranian people more than 100 times about the threat of them being used as human shields,” he said.

item.title: Iran Lets Chinese Ships Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions After Vessel Seizure
item.description: Iranian news agencies said some Chinese ships were being allowed through the strait, following diplomatic outreach to Iran from Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz early this month.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and U.A.E. Carried Out Secret Attacks in Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The Gulf Arab states have been grappling with how to deter Iran after the war made clear the limits of American security guarantees.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran, the capital of Iran, last week.

item.title: A Fact Check of Trump’s Claims on Inflation and Gas Prices in Iran War
item.description: He has minimized soaring gas prices, rising inflation and the American economy’s need for the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gas prices have risen about 53 percent higher than they were at the start of the war on Feb. 28.

item.title: Oil Prices Waver as Trump Meets China’s Xi to Discuss Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president is expected to urge the Chinese leader to help ease tensions in the Middle East by helping to persuade Iran to end the war.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel Says Netanyahu Made a Secret Trip to U.A.E., Which Says He Didn’t
item.description: The claim and the denial signaled both how close Israel and the United Arab Emirates have become, and how delicate that relationship remains.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem in April.

item.title: Gen. Caine’s Silence on Iran War Leaves Questions About Military Strategy
item.description: The president’s top military adviser is walking a tightrope as he leads the military through a divisive and unpopular war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: In public, Gen. Dan Caine has avoided discussing with lawmakers the broader U.S. military strategy.

item.title: Top Gaza Negotiator Urges Hamas to Embrace Rebuilding Plan
item.description: Nickolay Mladenov, who is overseeing the U.S.-led truce in Gaza, has tried to convince the militant group to give up its arms, but it has so far refused amid Israeli cease-fire violations.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Adam Rasgon and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Hamas in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Chinese Firms Plot Secret Arms Sales to Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The effort involves plans to send weapons through other countries in an effort to hide the origins of the shipments.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Mark Mazzetti and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes diplomatic visit with President Xi Jinping of China.

item.title: For Trump, the Iran War Looms Over China Summit
item.description: The balance of power between the United States and China had shifted in Beijing’s favor even before the war in Iran began in February.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump leaving the White House on Tuesday. He arrived in Beijing today.

item.title: Why the Bombing of Iran Tied the U.S. More Closely to China
item.description: As the U.S. tries to rebuild its weapons stockpiles drained in the Iran war, it will need access to rare-earth minerals, an industry China dominates.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A destroyed building in Tehran last month. The war in Iran is expected to figure into talks between the United States and China in Beijing this week.

item.title: At Cannes 2026, Gaza Becomes a Political Flashpoint
item.description: The event’s leaders immediately jumped into the debate over whether festival participants should speak out on issues like Israel and Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan
item.media:description: From left, the Cannes Film Festival president, Iris Knobloch, the jury president, Peter Jackson, and the festival director, Thierry Frémaux.

item.title: Trump Is in China as Iran War Continues With No End in Sight
item.description: Before leaving Washington on Tuesday, the president reiterated threats to decimate Iran if it doesn’t agree to a deal to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump departing Joint Base Andrews in Maryland for China on Tuesday.

item.title: Gulf Countries Arrest Shiite ‘Traitors’ Amid War With Iran
item.description: Dozens of Gulf citizens have been accused of belonging to Iran-linked terrorism cells as the war accelerates a shift toward deeper authoritarianism in the region.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke billowing after a drone strike in Kuwait City in March.
